title: Manufacturer Controlled DTC
system: Manufacturer Controlled DTC
possible_causes: Manufacturer Controlled DTC definition is manufacturer-controlled; exact diagnostic meaning is vehicle-specific.
Powertrain control module strategy, manufacturer-specific subsystem fault, wiring, sensor/actuator, calibration, or related DTC influence.
Do not infer cause, thresholds, terminals, or repair steps without the manufacturer service manual for the exact vehicle.
check_order: Save all DTCs, freeze-frame data, module information, calibration ID, readiness state, and related manufacturer-specific DTCs.
Identify the vehicle, engine, transmission, hybrid/EV system if equipped, and manufacturer-specific definition from the official service information.
Follow the manufacturer pinpoint test for terminals, thresholds, enable conditions, and inspection order.
Separate generic supporting DTCs from manufacturer-specific root cause using the official procedure.
common_mistakes: Treating manufacturer-controlled P3xxx definitions as universal.
Replacing parts based only on a generic title.
Skipping calibration, TSB, and service manual checks.
safety_notes: Use caution for any system named by the manufacturer procedure; high voltage, fuel, brake, SRS, hydraulic, rotating, and hot components require the exact manufacturer safety procedure.
source: SAE J2012 / SAE J2012DA generic DTC definitions and Autel official DTC table based diagnostic guidance

title: Cylinder Deactivation System Bank 1
system: Cylinder Deactivation System Bank 1
possible_causes: Cylinder Deactivation System Bank 1 fault in cylinder deactivation or intake/exhaust valve control.
Oil pressure/quality, valve control solenoid, wiring, connector, power/ground, lifter/rocker mechanism, PCM driver, cylinder deactivation strategy, or related DTC influence.
Cylinder numbering, bank layout, oil pressure thresholds, and enable conditions vary by engine and must be confirmed in the manufacturer service manual.
check_order: Save DTCs, freeze-frame data, oil temperature/pressure if available, cylinder deactivation command state, misfire data, valve control command feedback, and related DTCs.
Confirm vehicle-specific cylinder numbering, bank assignment, actuator/solenoid location, terminals, oil pressure requirements, enable conditions, and test steps in the manufacturer service manual.
Inspect engine oil level/condition/pressure, wiring, connectors, power/ground, actuator command, valve control solenoid operation, and related misfire or oil-pressure DTCs.
Separate electrical circuit, oil pressure, actuator/solenoid, mechanical valvetrain, PCM driver, and calibration causes using the manufacturer procedure.
common_mistakes: Assuming cylinder numbering, bank layout, or solenoid location across engines.
Replacing valve control parts without checking oil level, oil pressure, contamination, and related misfire/oil-pressure DTCs.
Commanding actuators without following service manual prerequisites and safety steps.
safety_notes: Use strong caution around hot engine oil, moving engine components, rotating belts/fans, and possible high-pressure lubrication circuits; disable/secure the vehicle as specified and follow the manufacturer procedure.
source: SAE J2012 / SAE J2012DA generic DTC definitions and Autel official DTC table based diagnostic guidance
